_scenario: SUSPICIOUS — multiple red flags: shipper/seller mismatch, declared origin inconsistent with port of loading, severely undervalued semiconductor goods, vague B/L description, Section 301 exposure
_red_flags: Shipper on B/L (Guangzhou Apex Trading Ltd, China) differs from invoice seller (Sunrise Global Exports Pte Ltd, Singapore)
Invoice declares country of origin as Malaysia, but port of loading is Yantian (Shenzhen), China
B/L description is 'General Merchandise' — vague, inconsistent with invoice description of semiconductor ICs
Declared value USD 6,800 for 8,000 integrated circuit boards is approximately USD 0.85/unit — market rate for comparable ICs is USD 4–12/unit (potential 80% undervaluation)
HTS 8542.31.0000 (monolithic integrated circuits) from China subject to Section 301 List 3 at 25%
Notify party is a freight forwarder with no discernible connection to the consignee
Payment terms: full TT advance payment to offshore account — atypical for legitimate commercial relationships of this value
